questions.question: What is the primary purpose of a database button in Notion?

options.text: To create a visual gallery of images
options.explanation: Database buttons are not for displaying images; they are for automating workflows.

options.text: To create a property that automates workflows
options.explanation: Database buttons are a type of database property that automates workflows, such as approving documents or sending notifications.

options.text: To restrict access to the database
options.explanation: Database buttons do not control access permissions; they control automation actions.

options.text: To link to external websites only
options.explanation: While 'Open page or URL' is one specific action, the button itself is a general automation property.

questions.question: Which symbol is used to create a formula within a database button action?

options.explanation: The @ symbol is used for mentions (dates, people, pages, groups), not formulas.
The ∑ symbol is used to create formulas in database button actions.
The # symbol is used for page properties or tags, not formulas.
The * symbol is used for bolding text in Markdown, not for creating formulas.

questions.question: Who is allowed to click a database button?

options.text: Anyone with Full access permissions
options.explanation: While Full access users can click, the permission is broader, including Can edit and Can edit content users.

options.text: Full access, Can edit, or Can edit content permissions
options.explanation: Users with these three specific permission levels can click database buttons.

options.text: Only users on the Pro plan
options.explanation: Clicking permission is available to specific access levels, not necessarily tied to plan tiers.

options.text: Only the owner of the database
options.explanation: The owner is included, but the rule applies to a wider group of editors as stated in the article.

questions.question: Which action is used to add a new page to a database and edit its properties automatically?

options.text: Edit property
options.explanation: Edit property only edits properties within the current database, it does not create new pages.

options.text: Add page to
options.explanation: The 'Add page to' action adds a page to a chosen database and edits the properties of that new page.

options.text: Send notification to
options.explanation: This action sends a notification, it does not create or edit pages.

options.text: Define variables
options.explanation: Define variables creates custom variables for use in other actions, it does not add pages.

questions.question: What is the maximum number of people a 'Send notification to' action can target?

options.text: Unlimited
options.explanation: There is a specific limit on the number of recipients for this action.
The 'Send notification to' action can send to up to 20 people in the workspace.
The limit is 20, not 5.

questions.question: Which of the following actions does NOT support using formulas?

options.text: Edit property
options.explanation: Formulas can be used in the 'Edit property' action.

options.text: Sending Slack notifications
options.explanation: The article explicitly states that formulas cannot be used in 'Sending Slack notifications' or 'Opening a page or URL'.

options.text: Define variables
options.explanation: Formulas are actually required to define variables.

options.text: Send mail to
options.explanation: Formulas can be used in the Message and Subject fields of the 'Send mail to' action.

questions.question: How do you add a database button property to an existing database?

options.text: Select ••• at the top right -> Properties -> New property -> Button
options.explanation: While this creates the button, the article specifies the steps start with the slider icon for the database view.

options.text: Select the slider icon at the top of a database -> Edit properties -> New property -> Button
options.explanation: This matches the exact step-by-step instructions provided in the 'Create a database button' section.

options.text: Right-click the database header -> Add property -> Button
options.explanation: There is no 'Add property' option in the right-click context menu for this specific workflow.

options.text: Drag a button block from the sidebar into the database
options.explanation: Database buttons are a property type, not a drag-and-drop block.

questions.question: What is the primary function of the 'Define variables' action?

options.text: To create a custom variable using mentions and formulas to be used in other actions
options.explanation: This action allows you to create a custom variable, which can then be utilized within other button actions.

options.text: To send an automated email
options.explanation: That is the function of the 'Send mail to' action.

options.text: To link an external database
options.explanation: That is the function of 'Add page to' or 'Edit pages in'.

options.text: To verify the user's identity
options.explanation: Identity verification is not a function of this specific action.

questions.question: Which account is required to use the 'Send mail to' action in database buttons?

options.text: Outlook account
options.explanation: The 'Send mail to' action specifically requires a Gmail account.

options.text: Gmail account
options.explanation: A Gmail account is required to use this automation, and it must be linked to the Notion account.

options.text: Notion AI account
options.explanation: Notion AI is used for creating formulas, but the email sender must be a Gmail account.

options.text: No account is required
options.explanation: An account is required; specifically, a Gmail account linked to the automation.

questions.question: If an automation runs into an error during setup, what happens?

options.text: The automation will run anyway after a delay
options.explanation: You will not be able to save the automation until errors are fixed.

options.text: You won't be able to save the automation until you make the required corrections
options.explanation: Notion provides error information during setup, but prevents saving until the issues are resolved.

options.text: The error is ignored and the button clicks immediately
options.explanation: The system prevents the automation from being saved if it contains errors.

options.text: The button is deleted automatically
options.explanation: The button property remains, but the automation configuration cannot be saved.

questions.question: Which plans are required to use the 'Send Slack notification to' action?

options.text: Free and Pro plans
options.explanation: Slack notifications are not available on the Free or Pro plans.

options.text: Plus, Business, and Enterprise plans
options.explanation: This feature is exclusively available on Plus, Business, and Enterprise plans.

options.text: Personal and Pro plans
options.explanation: These specific plans do not support this feature.

options.text: Any plan with a connected Slack workspace
options.explanation: The plan tier itself is a requirement, not just the connection.

questions.question: Which action opens a small confirmation screen before a page or database is changed?

options.text: Show confirmation
options.explanation: The 'Show confirmation' action opens a small confirmation screen to warn users before changes occur.

options.text: Open page or URL
options.explanation: This action simply opens a page or link without a confirmation screen.

options.text: Send notification to
options.explanation: This action sends a notification, it does not display a confirmation screen.

options.text: Edit property
options.explanation: This action directly edits the property without a confirmation step.

questions.question: Can mentions and formulas be used in the trigger of a database button?

options.text: Yes, but only if you select the slider icon
options.explanation: The article explicitly states they can only be used in button actions, not triggers.

options.text: No, they can only be used in button actions
options.explanation: Currently, mentions and formulas are restricted to button actions and cannot be used in triggers.

options.text: Yes, they can be used in any part of the button setup
options.explanation: There are specific restrictions on where these symbols can be used.

options.text: No, formulas cannot be used at all in database buttons
options.explanation: Formulas can be used in specific actions, just not in triggers.

questions.question: What happens if a database that an automation is trying to add to or edit is missing?

options.text: The automation will wait and try again later
options.explanation: If a database is missing, the automation will run into an error when it attempts the action.

options.text: The automation will send a notification to the user
options.explanation: While a notification might occur, the primary issue is that the automation cannot find the target database.

options.text: The automation runs into an error
options.explanation: Missing databases are one of the specific causes of runtime errors mentioned in the article.

options.text: The automation will create the missing database automatically
options.explanation: The automation cannot create a database that does not exist to edit or add to.

questions.question: When setting up the 'Send mail to' action, how is the 'Send replies to' field useful?

options.text: It sends the reply to the same person who triggered the button
options.explanation: By default, replies go to the sender; this field allows you to specify a different recipient.

options.text: It allows you to specify a different person to receive replies than the sender
options.explanation: This optional setting lets you route replies to a person property or a person in the workspace.

options.text: It is used to BCC the sender on their own email
options.explanation: BCC is handled separately via the CC/BCC field, not the 'Send replies to' field.

options.text: It is required for the email to be sent
options.explanation: This field is optional and only relevant if you want to change where replies are directed.
